observations: FIX 1 CONFIRMED: scope.files_create: [] is now present (030.json line 16). Reproduced live: `python3 -c 'jsonschema.validate(030.json, contract.schema.json)'` succeeds -- the contract now satisfies scope.required: ['files_create','files_modify']. `bash tools/validate.sh` full sweep prints `[C11] PASS dogfood` and final line `SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed` (was 10/1 in rev0 solely because C11 rejected the schema-invalid 030.json artifact). The rev0 issues[0] high-severity blocker is fully resolved; the false design_notes '11 passed, 0 failed' claim is now literally true on the tree WITH 030.json present.

FIX 2 CONFIRMED: the C8 mechanism for tools/README.md is now a FRONTMATTER change_log entry (version 0.3.0, date 2026-07-10), not a body note. Verified against C8's actual implementation (tools/validate.sh check_c8_version_changelog): C8 accepts ONLY (a) a current-frontmatter change_log entry whose version == the new version, OR (b) the literal file-path string appearing in CHANGELOG.md -- it never reads the target file's body prose. rev1's frontmatter change_log 0.3.0 entry hits branch (a) exactly. The false rev0 claim that a body note satisfies C8 / 'matches module convention' is removed from scope.files_modify[tools/README.md].purpose (line 20 now explicitly states 'the frontmatter change_log entry (not the body) is what satisfies C8'). The C1-C11 body enumeration is correctly retained but re-scoped to AT1's descriptive requirement, not C8.

MODULE PRECEDENT CONFIRMED: tools/SKILL.md (version 0.3.0) and tools/interface.md (version 0.3.0) both carry frontmatter change_log arrays and no body-note mechanism -- the rev1 approach matches the module's only two other version-bumped docs, resolving rev0 unsupported_claims[1]. tools/README.md at HEAD is version 0.2.0 with no change_log key; rev1 widens its frontmatter by adding the change_log array, consistent with sibling docs.

COVERAGE GAP CLOSED: new verification entry 17 (030.json lines 104-107) parses tools/README.md frontmatter to assert a version-0.3.0 change_log entry AND runs the DEFAULT (non---target) `bash tools/validate.sh` sweep asserting `[C8] PASS`. This directly proves C8 for tools/README.md in the mode that actually exercises it -- AT2's `validate.sh --target` structurally never invokes C8 (confirmed: the default-mode dispatcher runs check_c8_version_changelog on shipped_md; --target does not). The rev0 missing_acceptance_coverage entry is closed. The verification array is now 17 entries (AT1-AT11 = 11 acceptance tests + 6 hardening/anti-vacuity/non-goal guards), and evidence_convention (line 111) is updated to 17.

NO REGRESSION -- all rev0-sound items are byte-for-byte unchanged and re-verified: (1) v0.4.0->v0.6.0 release-gate correction (design_notes + AT8/entry 8) -- `git tag -l 'v0.6.0'` remains empty, gate is real and non-tautological; (2) NDEBT-002 read-only no-op (AT5/AT6 + byte-identical DEBT.md guard, entry 13) -- unchanged; (3) NIZAM.json held at 0.5.1 (non-goal + byte-identical guard, entry 12) -- protects C10 version-anchor; (4) CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] phase-004 entries (AT3/AT4) with the [Unreleased]-scoping regex -- unchanged; (5) untracked-aware scope guard (AT11) allow-list tools/README.md + CHANGELOG.md only; (6) C5 branding (AT10); (7) all nine non_goals. The evaluator's rev0 review independently re-executed and confirmed every one of these as sound and non-vacuous.

approved_at CORRECTLY OMITTED from approvals: the schema types approved_at as string-only when present, and prior contracts omit it pre-approval; 030.json validates cleanly without it. revisions bumped to 1. status remains 'proposed' -- correct Mode A input. Mode determined as A (status proposed, no git diff provided).

Non-blocking, pre-existing (unchanged from rev0): upstream tags v0.5.2/v0.5.3 exist beyond what CHANGELOG.md/NIZAM.json document -- pre-phase-004 debt outside feature 030's scope, does not affect any of the 17 verification commands. vlib_scope_guard allow-list is exact-or-prefix (not path-boundary-anchored) -- inherited F-023 library behavior, not introduced or worsened here.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. Both rev0 high-severity blockers are independently reproduced as fixed with zero regressions. FIX 1: scope.files_create: [] added -> 030.json now validates against schema/contract.schema.json (live jsonschema.validate succeeds), and `bash tools/validate.sh` returns SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed with [C11] PASS (was 10/1 because C11 dogfood-rejected the schema-invalid rev0 contract) -- the design_notes '11 passed, 0 failed' invariant is now literally true. FIX 2: the tools/README.md C8-satisfaction mechanism is switched from the empirically-insufficient body note to a frontmatter change_log entry (version 0.3.0), which is exactly one of the two forms C8's check_c8_version_changelog actually accepts (frontmatter change_log version-match OR literal CHANGELOG.md path mention; body prose is never read) and matches the module's own precedent (tools/SKILL.md + tools/interface.md both use frontmatter change_log arrays); the false 'body note satisfies C8' claim is removed. New verification entry 17 closes the rev0 coverage gap by parsing the README frontmatter for the 0.3.0 change_log entry AND asserting [C8] PASS on the DEFAULT sweep (the mode that actually runs C8, unlike AT2's C8-skipping --target). Verification array is now 17 entries (11 acceptance tests + 6 hardening guards); evidence_convention updated to 17; approved_at correctly omitted; revisions=1. Everything else the evaluator confirmed sound in rev0 -- the v0.6.0 gate correction (AT8), NDEBT-002 read-only confirmation (AT5/AT6 + byte-identical DEBT.md guard), NIZAM.json held at 0.5.1 (protecting C10's version anchor), the CHANGELOG [Unreleased] phase-004 entries, the untracked-aware scope guard, C5, and all nine non-goals -- is byte-identical and re-verified. Zero blocking issues, zero missing acceptance coverage, zero unsupported claims. Contract may proceed to implementation (Loop-2).
